name: worktree-helper
description: Makes each git worktree window visually distinct and context-aware: titlebar color, branch/color env vars, and auto-opened terminals.

commands.title: Worktree Helper: Clean Up This Window

worktreeHelper.autoApply.markdownDescription: Automatically apply worktree config (color, env, terminals) when a linked worktree window is opened.

worktreeHelper.applyToMainWorktree.markdownDescription: Also apply config to the primary working tree, not just linked worktrees.

worktreeHelper.setupCommands.markdownDescription: Commands to run **once** when a worktree is first set up (e.g. `npm install`, copying untracked config). They run sequentially in the worktree folder as VS Code tasks, stopping at the first failure, and re-run on the next open until they all succeed. Unlike `#worktreeHelper.terminals#`, these are expected to finish — use terminals for long-running processes like dev servers. Re-run on demand with the *Worktree Helper: Run Setup Commands* command.

worktreeHelper.terminals.markdownDescription: Terminals to open in the worktree. Each runs in the worktree folder, then executes `command`. Omit `command` (or leave it empty) to open a plain terminal with nothing run.

command.description: Command to run when the terminal opens. Omit or leave empty to open a plain terminal.

worktreeHelper.palette.markdownDescription: Candidate titlebar background colors (hex). New worktrees pick the most distinct unused color from this list.

worktreeHelper.colorOverride.markdownDescription: Force a specific titlebar background color (hex) for this workspace. Leave empty to auto-pick.

worktreeHelper.injectEnv.markdownDescription: Inject `GIT_BRANCH` and the titlebar color into integrated terminals.

worktreeHelper.writeEnvFile.markdownDescription: Write a git-excluded `.env` file at the worktree root so debuggers and tasks can read the vars.

worktreeHelper.branchEnvVar.description: Environment variable name holding the branch name.

worktreeHelper.colorEnvVar.description: Environment variable name holding the titlebar color.

worktreeHelper.openTerminals.markdownDescription: Open the configured `worktreeHelper.terminals` when a worktree is set up.

worktreeHelper.gitExcludeWrites.markdownDescription: Add files we write (`.vscode/settings.json`, `.env`) to `.git/info/exclude` so they don't pollute git.
